refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve the customer-defined teleoperation link availability target.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is mostly atomic, focusing on achieving a specific teleoperation link availability. The phrase 'customer-defined' keeps it solution-neutral by not specifying how availability is achieved. The FR is kept in the problem domain by stating what must be achieved (link availability) without implying any implementation details.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the teleoperation link availability over the agreed evaluation period and verify it meets or exceeds the customer-defined target (e.g., ≥ 0.999).
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall achieve the customer-defined teleoperation link availability target.

candidate_dps: Implement redundant communication channels to ensure continuous link availability
Incorporate real-time link health monitoring and automatic failover mechanisms
Design adaptive bandwidth management to prioritize teleoperation traffic
Establish robust error detection and correction protocols for the communication link
Utilize predictive analytics to anticipate and mitigate link degradation
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement redundant communication channels to ensure continuous link availability

potential_sources: Dependency on single communication channel technology
Failover mechanisms tightly integrated with specific hardware
coupling_check.notes: Selecting redundant communication channels as DP promotes independence by isolating failure domains and enabling modular failover strategies, reducing coupling between components.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of multiple independent communication paths at the worksite
Risk that redundancy may increase system complexity and cost
Potential operational policy constraints limiting certain communication methods
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including monitoring and automatic failover as secondary DPs to complement redundancy and enhance robustness.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the overall communication infrastructure and management, including redundant communication channels to ensure continuous link availability.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Handles the physical and logical network connections, including redundant channels for continuous availability.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Sends status and health data over the communication channels.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as redundant communication channels are core to NGHE Solution; minimal diffusion outside this module.
Vehicle uses communication but does not implement redundancy logic; would cause diffusion of communication logic.
Teleoperation depends on communication but does not manage redundancy; placing DP here would diffuse communication concerns.
Safety Services rely on communication but are not responsible for channel redundancy; poor containment.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and appropriate boundary to contain the DP of implementing redundant communication channels, as it directly manages communication infrastructure and ensures continuous link availability.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Vehicle module if vehicle-specific communication hardware requires coordination.
Interface complexity with Teleoperation and Safety Services if redundancy status needs to be propagated.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP of implementing redundant communication channels directly addresses the FR of achieving the customer-defined teleoperation link availability target by ensuring continuous link availability, which aligns well with the acceptance criteria of measured link availability ≥ 0.999.

diffusion_risks: Potential overlap with Vehicle module if vehicle-specific communication hardware requires coordination.
Interface complexity with Teleoperation and Safety Services if redundancy status needs to be propagated.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 1), indicating that the DP is well contained within this module. Other modules show higher diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the ownership candidate.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through monitoring the link availability metrics and telemetry data sent via the NGHE Solution module interfaces, ensuring the customer-defined target is met.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution IM boundary is clear and appropriate, as it encompasses the communication infrastructure and redundancy logic necessary to fulfill the FR.

recommended_revisions: Clarify interface definitions and coordination mechanisms between NGHE Solution and Vehicle module to mitigate overlap risks.
Define explicit protocols for propagating redundancy status to Teleoperation and Safety Services modules to manage interface complexity and avoid diffusion.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with clear alignment, containment, and verification feasibility. However, given the high safety criticality and identified diffusion risks related to module overlap and interface complexity, it is prudent to further decompose the FR into next-level sub-FRs to explicitly address these concerns and enable more granular design and verification.
next_level_sub_frs: Ensure continuous link availability through redundant communication channels
Coordinate communication hardware interfaces with Vehicle module
Propagate redundancy status to Teleoperation and Safety Services modules

revise.why: To clarify interface definitions and coordination mechanisms between NGHE Solution and Vehicle module, and to define explicit protocols for propagating redundancy status to Teleoperation and Safety Services modules, mitigating overlap and interface complexity risks.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module is the primary owner of the communication redundancy DP.
High containment score indicates good modularization but interface risks remain.
Verification through telemetry data is reliable and feasible.
open_risks: Potential overlap with Vehicle module communication hardware.
Interface complexity with Teleoperation and Safety Services modules.
Propagation of redundancy status may cause diffusion if not well defined.
evidence_needed: Detailed interface specifications between NGHE Solution and Vehicle module.
Verification plans for sub-FRs and their allocated DPs and IMs.
